subject: Sociology
title: Religious Fundamentalism
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Religious Fundamentalism.

hook.text: In a world of rapid change, some believers say the answer is simple: go back to the original text.

buildup.text: Fundamentalism is a modern reaction to modernity. It demands literal return to sacred texts and traditions.

discovery.text: Sociologists see it as a response to uncertainty. When the world shifts fast, rigid answers feel safe.

twist.text: Fundamentalism exists in every major religion. It's not about one faith — it's a pattern of reaction.

climax.text: Fundamentalism is both conservative and revolutionary. It resists modernity while using modern tools.

quiz.question: What drives religious fundamentalism according to sociologists?
options: Reaction to rapid social change and uncertainty
